characters.name: Stephen Addison
characters.description: Stephen Addisonis a character portrayed byHouston Rhinesin theABCcrime seriesCastle. Stephen is the son and heir of Randolph Addison, and the brother ofLyla Addison, the latest in a long line of prominent, powerful New Yorkers to bear this name. He is seen in "Law & Murder", whereOtis Williamsis on trial for the murder of Lyla, and the D.A. himself,Lou Karnacki, is prosecuting the case. Juror Number Seven,Joe McUsic, drops dead in the courtroom, and the "clearly guilty" Williams looks as if he might literally get away with murder. The true tale, however, reveals the sybaritic, destructive, petulant children that Stephen and his sister have been, his accidental shooting of her during a drug run, and subsequent entirely premeditated murder of McUsic.Eddie McUsic, Stephen's unwilling accomplice in the first death, sums him up in these words: "This little trust-fund gangster, thinks he's so badass."

characters.name: Stephen Addison

characters.name: Lyla Addison
characters.description: Lyla Addisonwas the scion of an extremely rich and influential New York family, who was found dead in the trunk of her car by police officers in a case which initiated the events of "Law & Murder". The members of the jury in the trial ofOtis Williamsfor Lyla's murder includedJoe McUsic, who had pertinent information confirming Williams' innocence, and bribed his way onto the jury to try to prevent a miscarriage of justice. McUsic could not simply come forward with the evidence because his brother,Eddie, was entangled in the events. Evidence would show that Lyla was, in point of fact, killed by her own brother,Stephen, in an accidental shooting when their car hit a pothole during a drug run. He also murdered McUsic in his attempt to cover up his crime.

characters.name: Lyla Addison

characters.name: Adler
characters.description: AgentAdlerwas the partner of AgentWinfieldin the D.O.J. operation to keep Mafia witnessJimmy Moransafe in his hospital room. She hadMaggie Dowdon her list of those with access, and cleared her to send "A Death in the Family" into its rinse cycle.

characters.name: Adler
characters.description: AgentAdlerwas the partner of AgentWinfieldin the D.O.J. operation to keep Mafia witnessJimmy Moransafe in his hospital room. She hadMaggie Dowdon her list of those with access, and cleared her to send "A Death in the Family" into its rinse cycle.

characters.name: Agent Harris
characters.description: Agent Harrisis an FBI Agent who specializing in missing persons. He was the agent assigned toAlexisandSara El-Masri's kidnapping. Harris aided in finding and rescuing Sara El-Masri and Alexis. Like everyone else, he thought that Sara El Masri was the target but when it was really Alexis that was the target. He andBeckettsteam determined that the missing girls were in Paris. Eventually, the kidnappers released Sara El-Masri after the ransom was paid and later Alexis was rescued byCastleand Castle's Father, who was using the alias ofJackson Hunt.

characters.name: Agent Jones
characters.description: Agent Jonesis a CIA agent. He was the one who takes Castle and Beckett, blindfolded, to a secure CIA facility and assists them, as well asSophia TurnerandMartin Danberg, in catching rogue CIA operativeThomas Gage.

characters.name: Agent Jones
characters.description: Agent Jonesis a CIA agent. He was the one who takes Castle and Beckett, blindfolded, to a secure CIA facility and assists them, as well asSophia TurnerandMartin Danberg, in catching rogue CIA operativeThomas Gage.

characters.name: Agent Trahn
characters.description: Agent Trahnis a character portrayed byDavid Limin theABCcrime seriesCastle. Agent Trahn is the first layer in the smokescreen created bySophia Turnerto hide her own turncoat tendencies, and appears at first to be a beard employed byAgent Danbergto mask his own nefarious computer activities.

characters.name: Lissa Akerman
characters.description: Lissa Akermanis a character portrayed byAllyssa Mauricein theABCcrime seriesCastle. Dr. Lissa Akerman is a pharmacist at County Hospital, and one of the many planets in whatRyandubs the "Galaxy of Greg". The eponymousGreg McClintockis a nurse at County who, by wild coincidence, is considered their "special friend" by practically every woman on staff. This does not, to Castle's disappointment, have the makings of a "Catfight to the Death", as they are all, genuinely, "just friends" - no sex involved.

characters.name: Amir Alhabi
characters.description: Amir Alhabiwas the unfortunate soul whose American Dream transitioned into the long goodnight, when he was selected as a patsy by a group of "patriots" in need of a Middle Eastern scapegoat for their terrorist attack. His body is the eponymous "Setup" of the selfsame episode, and as his story unfolds, it becomes clear that this was a highly intelligent, hard-working family man, the epitome of the values his killers claim to represent. Amir has a wife,Nazihahand a baby daughter, and was in business, as a cabbie, with his cousinJamal. In order to supplement his income, since he owned his own medallion, Amir also rented out shifts in his cab toKevin McCann, another cabbie, who worked around Amir's own use.

characters.name: Amir Alhabi
characters.description: Amir Alhabiwas the unfortunate soul whose American Dream transitioned into the long goodnight, when he was selected as a patsy by a group of "patriots" in need of a Middle Eastern scapegoat for their terrorist attack. His body is the eponymous "Setup" of the selfsame episode, and as his story unfolds, it becomes clear that this was a highly intelligent, hard-working family man, the epitome of the values his killers claim to represent. Amir has a wife,Nazihahand a baby daughter, and was in business, as a cabbie, with his cousinJamal. In order to supplement his income, since he owned his own medallion, Amir also rented out shifts in his cab toKevin McCann, another cabbie, who worked around Amir's own use.

characters.name: Jamal Alhabi
characters.description: Jamal Alhabiwas the cousin ofAmir, with whom he shared a cab and medallion. Jamal, as "Setup" sets off, has recently started his own moving company. Amir, although a hardworking cabbie now, was, back in the day, a Syrian nuclear physicist, and this tangled web of past and present makes him and Jamal extremely viable suspects in the bomb plot whichBeckettand the team uncover. Jamal in particular is seen wheeling a large, heavy load out of a storage unit, a load which evidence suggests may well be a WMD, and he seems to have MMO where Amir's death is concerned. In the end, all members of the Alhabi family are exonerated - they were the sadly convenient scapegoats for the true killer,Radford Hayes, and his cohorts. Jamal was killed by the so-called "patriots" when he tried to remove their bomb.

characters.name: Jamal Alhabi

characters.name: Nazihah Alhabi
characters.description: Nazihah Alhabihas the misfortune to be a young widow with a newborn as "Setup" unfolds. Her husband,Amir Alhabi, was the victim whose case precipitated the events of this two-parter, being a Syrian-American with a background as a nuclear physicist. He was being set up as a scapegoat by a terrorist cell who wanted a plausible Middle-Eastern mastermind for their bomb plot. Nazihah's woes only begin with his death, however, as DHS AgentMark Fallondecides to play a severe game of "bad cop, good cop" by threatening to take her baby away unless she tells them the truth about her husband, and waiting forBeckettto step in. Later still, the plotters kidnap Nazihah's daughter and force her to drive their "dirty bomb" into New York, hoping to salvage their plan by her coerced involvement. Thankfully,Castleand Beckett are able to intercept Nazihah before she can reach her destination, informing her that they rescued her baby and arrested the men responsible.

characters.name: John Allen
characters.description: John Allenis an unemployed former insurance salesman who turns up deceased in a tree at the opening of "Deep in Death". He then appears again, still dead, in an alleyway with his internal organs removed, since as the coroner's van was taking him away it was hijacked at gunpoint by masked men. John, it turns out, had failed to inform his wife,Sandy, when he lost his job months before, and out of desperation he took a gig as a drug mule. This involved swallowing balloons filled with the drugs to bring them over the border from Mexico. His 'sponsors' discovered he was missing with their product and assumed the worst - that he'd done a runner - but when they saw his body in the tree and cops all around they had to re-strategize. John had tried to pull himself out of his troubles without 'bothering' his wife, but sadly, the consequences were likely to prove lifelong for both of them.

characters.name: Sandy Allen
characters.description: Sandy Allenis the widow of the lateJohn Allenas "Deep in Death" opens its tale. Sandy and her husband were struggling financially to keep their heads above water after he lost his job - and failed to tell her. Sandy was convinced he was still working in his office in midtown, when the reality was that he'd been unemployed for eight months. John's love for the family, herself and their children, is something Sandy felt strongly, and she flies to his defense whenCastleimplies that his "working late" might have been... something else. His strong conviction that handouts are bad may have led to him falling in with drug dealers to make ends meet. Sandy givesBeckettand Castle their first suspect,Max Haverstock, who apparently called their apartment late at night. Sandy believed this was to lambast her husband, who she thought had fired Max, whereas in truth they had both lost their jobs the same day.

characters.name: Alma
characters.description: Almahad a quiet, shytenant, who seemed to believe he was a vampire. As someone who'd been married to a Civil War re-enactor, Alma took this in her stride. Sadly, by the events of "Vampire Weekend", Matthew was no more, and trying to discern where the Crow flew from,BeckettandCastledescend on Alma and have her show them his place. Matthew's talent as an artist is on display here, all around the wall, and Alma also provides the tidbit that another vamp, this time female, had visited recently, and got into a big fight with Matthew.

characters.name: Amanda
characters.description: Amandais a character portrayed byMichelle Pagein theABCcrime seriesCastle. Amanda, a senior at Redding Prep, an exclusive New York private school, plays an active role in the tale a group of friends weave forBeckettandCastle. Away from her support network though, she shows most of the classic, looking-for-a-place-in-the-world, qualities of adolescence. Amanda is the ex ofDonny Kendalland the girlfriend ofBrandon, but her multiple, highly visible, piercings suggest she's trying to prove a point to someone in the world of "Hedge Fund Homeboys". Amanda provides Beckett with vital corroboration that the video of the fatal night that she shot was originally Brandon's idea. characters.name: Amanda Lowery
characters.description: Amanda Lowery(formerlyCollier) is the killer in the episode "Scared to Death", with a twist that freezes her victims in shocks of absolute fear. EvokingThe Ring, she would send her victims tapes with disturbing mixed images saying they only had three days left to live, after which Amanda killed them in a way which their expressions would show the intended reaction, fear, to their last sights - her once she revealed who she was. Amanda went after specific people with a tragic secret she was connected to. Her father, David Collier, was a suspect in the case of serial killerNigel Malloyyears ago, and the police were stressing to find the perpetrator. Fingering the wrong man, they brought Collier in for a lineup due to his mere history of insanity. After coercing four witnesses,Jason Bennett,Val Butler,Mark Heller, and a an unnamed person never seen on-screen, the group falsely identified Collier as being present at the scenes of the murders to the police. He was placed in lockup for three days, devastated and paranoid over being imprisoned for a crime he didn't commit. Collier's madness reached a peak in three days to the point where he fatally electrocuted himself in his holding cell. Malloy was never identified nor captured, while Collier's grieving family, daughter Amanda and her widowed mother, never recovered from the injustice which led to the loss of someone they loved.

characters.name: Amy Barrett
characters.description: Amy Barrettoriginally seems to have an uncanny cosmetic resemblance to dead patientSusan Watts, both having appointments with Dr.Kelly Nieman, esteemed surgeon and serial killerJerry Tyson's girlfriend. Barrett is persistently warned by DetectiveKate Beckettto watch herself, as with Watts already dead in Tyson's MO, she could be next, with Nieman setting her looks up for such a murder. However, after both taunts and stonewalling from Nieman and Tyson, Tyson going by the name Mike Boudreaux, a "truck driver", Barrett's revealed to be in collusion with them. In "Resurrection", she calls Beckett and claims she fears for her life, but at the meeting location, Barrett blitzes Beckett with an injection to knock her out and then places her in a wheelchair to abduct her. Calling Nieman, Barrett says, "It's done" in a garbled voice as she and Tyson leave the precinct uncharged. In "Reckoning", while the chase is on to save Beckett, Barrett keeps watch at the cabin Tyson's hiding at. While Nieman plans to exchange her face and Beckett's in a transplantation, Castle tracks the cabin and surprises Barrett after she steps outside for a smoke, performing a citizen's arrest. Barrett reveals Beckett's not there and Tyson's alone, with nothing wired on the house, before she's forced into the trunk of the car.

characters.name: Frank Anderson
characters.description: Frank Andersonis a murder victim that Ryan and Esposito are assigned to investigate in "The Double Down". Frank was killed byJason Cosway, cause of death being a single gunshot wound to the midsection in the back. Cosway staged the incident to look like a mugging gone wrong. Frank was the father ofChristina Marx, whose husbandEric'traded murders' with Cosway, killing Cosway's wifeAshleywhilst the other man established a rock-solid alibi. Eric wanted Frank's rent-controlled apartment, which would stay that way if the lease was taken over by a family member, i.e. his wife. He had also borrowed money from his father-in-law, which Frank was pressuring him to pay back.

characters.name: Jason Avery
characters.description: Jason Averyis a character portrayed byLeonard Robertsin theABCcrime seriesCastle. Avery was an FBI agent who was partnered with expert profilerJordan Shaw, maintaining a good friendship with her as well as an effective professional dynamic. He worked with the team from the12th Precinctwhile investigating serial killerScott Dunn, although he did miss out on Dunn's capture, as he was investigating a fake trap that Dunn had set for them. He displays very high-level data manipulation and gathering skills when employing the FBI version of the murder board, and is able to multitask very efficiently.

characters.name: Azi
characters.description: Aziis one of men that live in the rooftop apartment where murder victimJamal Buonsilives too. He was interrogated by Beckett and Castle.

characters.name: Greta Bailey
characters.description: Greta Baileyis the officer who walksBeckettandCastlethrough the crime scene which opens "The Final Nail". Greta appears to be a highly composed and organized individual, suggesting that she is very familiar with such sights as dead bodies. She has anticipated and accommodated all of the more obvious items on Beckett's checklist, a fact which, though not acknowledged verbally, seems to impress the detective.

characters.name: Stan Banks
characters.description: Stan Banksis the murder victim in "The Blue Butterfly", a former accountant turned treasure hunter. He seems to have lived a largely blameless life, but unfortunately his driven search for the bejewelled necklace took him way out of his depth, and he was killed. When Stan found a Spanish dubloon, it inspired him to become a treasure hunter. His obsession with his new occupation resulted in his marriage separation. Eventually seeing professional treasure hunterClyde Bellasco's documentary on his search for the Blue Butterfly necklace that went missing in 1947 inspired Stan to search for it himself. He learned that the necklace was last seen at the Pennybaker Club. Somehow, he deduced that the last people to have possessed it were P.I. Joe Flynn and Vera Mulqueen, mobsterTom Dempsey's moll, reportedly shot and burned in Joe's car. He tracked down Joe's secretary's granddaughter and purchased Joe's journal from her, as well as reviewed the old police file regarding Joe and Vera's murder. Stan later met withTom Dempsey III, the old mobster's grandson, posing a biographer under an pseudonym, wanting to document Dempsey the First's good side. Eventually, club singer Betsy Sinclair, who used to hang around the Pennybaker Club, passed away and Stan attended her funeral. While there, he bumped intoJerry Maddoxand questioned his relationship to Sinclair. Jerry claimed that he was a bartender at the club. Stan then had to leave when Dempsey the Third spotted him. Stan later met with Bellasco and told him all his findings, things that not even the latter knew about, but left again, as Dempsey was following him to see what he was up to. Stan later deduced that he was Joe and his wife Viola was Vera, having faked their deaths. He confronted the old couple, threatening to expose them if they didn't tell him the location of the Blue Butterfly. Once they told him its hiding place, Stan managed to recover the jewel, only to find Bellasco, staking out the place and not wanting Stan to seize his glory. being chloroformed by the Maddoxs' caretaker, Frankie Hunsacker, who was also the Joe's secretary's great-grandson. Frankie had been secretly and slowly attempting to get the necklace too from the Maddoxs and was angry at how easily Stan bullied it out of them. Stan wrestling with Frankie over the gun and accidentally shot.

characters.name: Stan Banks
characters.description: Stan Banksis the murder victim in "The Blue Butterfly", a former accountant turned treasure hunter. He seems to have lived a largely blameless life, but unfortunately his driven search for the bejewelled necklace took him way out of his depth, and he was killed. When Stan found a Spanish dubloon, it inspired him to become a treasure hunter. His obsession with his new occupation resulted in his marriage separation. Eventually seeing professional treasure hunterClyde Bellasco's documentary on his search for the Blue Butterfly necklace that went missing in 1947 inspired Stan to search for it himself. He learned that the necklace was last seen at the Pennybaker Club. Somehow, he deduced that the last people to have possessed it were P.I. Joe Flynn and Vera Mulqueen, mobsterTom Dempsey's moll, reportedly shot and burned in Joe's car. He tracked down Joe's secretary's granddaughter and purchased Joe's journal from her, as well as reviewed the old police file regarding Joe and Vera's murder. Stan later met withTom Dempsey III, the old mobster's grandson, posing a biographer under an pseudonym, wanting to document Dempsey the First's good side. Eventually, club singer Betsy Sinclair, who used to hang around the Pennybaker Club, passed away and Stan attended her funeral. While there, he bumped intoJerry Maddoxand questioned his relationship to Sinclair. Jerry claimed that he was a bartender at the club. Stan then had to leave when Dempsey the Third spotted him. Stan later met with Bellasco and told him all his findings, things that not even the latter knew about, but left again, as Dempsey was following him to see what he was up to. Stan later deduced that he was Joe and his wife Viola was Vera, having faked their deaths. He confronted the old couple, threatening to expose them if they didn't tell him the location of the Blue Butterfly. Once they told him its hiding place, Stan managed to recover the jewel, only to find Bellasco, staking out the place and not wanting Stan to seize his glory. being chloroformed by the Maddoxs' caretaker, Frankie Hunsacker, who was also the Joe's secretary's great-grandson. Frankie had been secretly and slowly attempting to get the necklace too from the Maddoxs and was angry at how easily Stan bullied it out of them. Stan wrestling with Frankie over the gun and accidentally shot.

characters.name: Patty Barker
characters.description: Dr. Patty Barkeris Royal the dog's therapist on "Castle" inseason 4in the episode "An Embarrassment of Bitches". Dr. Barker had a weekly appointment withFrancisco Pilaron his calendar and had been seeing Pilar's dog Royal for years. Her treatment involved close interaction with Royal, going as far as getting down on the floor and rolling around with him. Dr. Barker generally shows more concern and empathy for the dog than for the fact that his human master has been murdered.

characters.name: Kayla Baron
characters.description: Kayla Baronis a notable character that appeared onSeason 3episode "Pretty Dead". Kayla is the wife of business mogulVictor Baron- both are the sponsors of Baron's All-American Beauty Pageant. Kayla herself was a beauty queen and that is how she met her husband. When one of their contestants,Amber Middleberry, is murdered, she and her husband are the first ones interviewed byBeckettandCastle, but both Barons have solid alibis at the time of the murder.

characters.name: Victor Baron
characters.description: Victor Baronis a notable character who appeared onSeason 3episode "Pretty Dead". Baron a business mogul and is the sponsor of Baron's All American Beauty Pageant. His wife isKayla Baron, who was a beauty queen and now is his co-sponsor of the contest. When one of their contestants,Amber Middleberry, is murdered, Victor and Kayla are the first ones interviewed byBeckettandCastle. Both have strong alibis for the time of the murder; Victor tells Castle and Beckett that Candace Ford will give any information they need.
